---
title: "Supplement: Data Thinning Illustration (6 Maps)"
output:
  html_document:
    toc: false
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE,
                      fig.width = 14, fig.height = 8)
library(tidyverse)
library(sf)
library(tigris)
library(patchwork)
options(tigris_use_cache = TRUE)
```

Shows how data thinning splits one direct estimate $(z_i, d_i)$ into two
unbiased estimates $(z_{1i}, d_i/\varepsilon)$ and $(z_{2i}, d_i/(1-\varepsilon))$.

One sample from the 1.75% proportional allocation design, $\varepsilon = 0.7$.

```{r load_and_thin}
comp_dir <- "../_results_prop1p75pct/comparison_001"
z_obj <- readRDS(file.path(comp_dir, "z.RDS"))
d_obj <- readRDS(file.path(comp_dir, "d.RDS"))

z        <- z_obj$values
d        <- d_obj$values
puma_ids <- z_obj$puma
eps      <- 0.7

# Thinning (matches run_dt.R):
#   z_train = eps*z + sqrt(eps*(1-eps)*d) * xi
#   z_train ~ N(eps*theta, eps*d)
#   z_test  = z - z_train ~ N((1-eps)*theta, (1-eps)*d)
# Rescaled to be unbiased for theta:
#   z1 = z_train / eps       ~ N(theta, d/eps)
#   z2 = z_test  / (1-eps)   ~ N(theta, d/(1-eps))
set.seed(42)
noise   <- rnorm(length(z))
z_train <- eps * z + sqrt(eps * (1 - eps) * d) * noise
z_test  <- z - z_train
z1      <- z_train / eps
z2      <- z_test  / (1 - eps)

# Verify reconstruction: eps*z1 + (1-eps)*z2 = z
cat(sprintf("Max reconstruction error: %.2e\n", max(abs(eps*z1 + (1-eps)*z2 - z))))
cat(sprintf("n PUMAs: %d,  z range: [%.3f, %.3f]\n", length(z), min(z), max(z)))
```

```{r get_geometry}
ca_pumas <- pumas(state = "CA", cb = TRUE, year = 2020)

df <- tibble(
  PUMA   = puma_ids,
  z      = z,
  z1     = z1,
  z2     = z2,
  d      = d,
  d_eps  = d / eps,
  d_1eps = d / (1 - eps)
)

map_data <- ca_pumas %>%
  mutate(PUMA = PUMACE20) %>%
  inner_join(df, by = "PUMA")

cat(sprintf("Matched %d of %d PUMAs\n", nrow(map_data), length(puma_ids)))
```

```{r six_maps, fig.width=13, fig.height=6}
zlim <- range(c(map_data$z, map_data$z1, map_data$z2))
dlim <- range(c(map_data$d, map_data$d_eps, map_data$d_1eps))

make_map <- function(data, fill_var, title, limits, palette) {
  ggplot(data) +
    geom_sf(aes(fill = .data[[fill_var]]), color = "gray40", linewidth = 0.08) +
    scale_fill_distiller(palette = palette, direction = 1,
                         limits = limits, name = NULL,
                         breaks = scales::pretty_breaks(n = 3)) +
    labs(title = title) +
    theme_void() +
    theme(
      plot.title        = element_text(size = 12, hjust = 0.5, margin = margin(b = 4)),
      legend.position   = "right",
      legend.key.width  = unit(0.35, "cm"),
      legend.key.height = unit(1.8, "cm"),
      legend.text       = element_text(size = 8)
    )
}

p1 <- make_map(map_data, "z",      expression(y[i]),                   zlim, "Blues")
p2 <- make_map(map_data, "z1",     expression(y[i]^{(1)}/epsilon),     zlim, "Blues")
p3 <- make_map(map_data, "z2",     expression(y[i]^{(2)}/(1-epsilon)), zlim, "Blues")
p4 <- make_map(map_data, "d",      expression(d[i]),                   dlim, "Oranges")
p5 <- make_map(map_data, "d_eps",  expression(d[i]/epsilon),           dlim, "Oranges")
p6 <- make_map(map_data, "d_1eps", expression(d[i]/(1-epsilon)),       dlim, "Oranges")

row1 <- (p1 | p2 | p3) + plot_layout(guides = "collect") &
  theme(legend.position = "right")
row2 <- (p4 | p5 | p6) + plot_layout(guides = "collect") &
  theme(legend.position = "right")

row1 / row2 +
  plot_annotation(
    subtitle = bquote("One sample, 1.75% proportional allocation design," ~ epsilon ~ "= 0.7"),
    theme = theme(
      plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 11, hjust = 0.5, color = "gray40")
    )
  )
```

```{r save}
ggsave("plot_supplement_thinning_maps.png", width = 15, height = 9, dpi = 200)
```
